nodes.label: AI as brainstorming and research accelerator
nodes.label_negative: AI as crutch that reduces critical thinking
nodes.grounding_span: I primarily use it for brainstorming

nodes.label: AI for mechanical tasks (typos, grammar, coding)
nodes.grounding_span: I feel very okay with AI being used to fix my typos or grammatical issues

nodes.label: AI as glorified search engine
nodes.label_negative: AI as autonomous creator or collaborator
nodes.grounding_span: I think if I personally restrict AI to being a glorified search engine in work, then it is acceptable

nodes.label: AI reliability for objective tasks
nodes.label_negative: AI unreliability for subjective or creative tasks
nodes.grounding_span: For objective cases, I feel that AIs are fairly reliable, but when it comes to being conversational or discussing opinions, it is strange

nodes.label: AI voice human-likeness
nodes.label_negative: AI voice non-human to protect youth mental health
nodes.grounding_span: I think it is actually fairly crucial that AI sound non-human if it reads out loud

nodes.label: Personal development and critical thinking
nodes.grounding_span: not having AI forces brainstorming and critical thinking and research

nodes.label: Pedagogical responsibility and role modeling
nodes.grounding_span: I'm supposed to teach, not to be the messenger of the AI

nodes.label: Efficiency and productivity
nodes.grounding_span: It just is a helpful starting point that is faster than going through a separate search engine

nodes.label: Youth mental health and ethical safety
nodes.grounding_span: ethically I see major concerns for the mental health of youth who use AI with too-human sounding voices

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for brainstorming and resource finding
nodes.grounding_span: I felt particularly excited when in that recent lesson planning it gave me examples of circuit problems

nodes.label: Frustration with AI for subjective or creative tasks
nodes.grounding_span: The uses that frustrate me more are generally with respect to non-work related uses, such as creative writing or philosophical debates

nodes.label: Concern about AI reducing critical thinking in youth
nodes.grounding_span: recent studies have shown that AI basically reduces brain connectivity and general critical thinking and creativity

nodes.label: Intentional restriction of AI use to avoid crutch
nodes.grounding_span: I actively consider not using it as a crutch

nodes.label: Self-imposed boundaries and deliberate restriction
nodes.grounding_span: I generally try to do my own brainstorming or problem solving first

nodes.label: Task-specific calibration based on personal skill and value
nodes.grounding_span: I think the only real threats I see to my skills are in things I actively find frustrating
